english: please, do come! ₒkrid (khrid) dgós-pai (dgos pa'i) ysól-ba (gsol ba), or źú-ba (zhu ba), a request to be taken along with (by another person) Mil. C.: to wish, Kyọ' (Khyo') śe-pa (she pa) ₒdi ('di) ṅa (nga) go-pa (go ba) yin (yin) I wish you to know this Lew. II. sbst. necessity, want, use, purpose (W. dgós-ce (dgos ce), pronounced *gó-śe(s)* (*go she(s)*), máṅ-po (mang po) ₒtsol ('tsol) dgós-pa (dgos pa) byuṅ (byung) we have been under the necessity of looking for you a long time Mil.; ṅá-la (nga la) yyui (g.yu'i) dgós-pa (dgos pa) med (med) I have no use for that turkois, I do not want it Mil.; *tiṅ-la (ting la) gó-śe (go she) ₒpí-la* ('pi la*) W. for future use; dgós-pai (dgos pa'i) cuṅ-bas (cung bas) as it is rather useless; dgós-pa (dgos pa) cii (ci'i) pẏir (phyir) for what purpose? frq. III. adj. (C. also *gó-gyu* (*go gyu*), and *gọ* (*go*), W. *gó-śe* (*go she*), as in II.), necessary, due, needful, useful, med (med) kyaṅ (kyang) dgos-pai (dgos pa'i) ₒkral-bsdud ('khral bsdud) a tax necessarily to be paid, unrelentingly exacted Mil.; ráṅ-la (rang la) dgós-pai (dgos pa'i) skál-ba (skal ba) the portion due to you Mil.; dgós-pai (dgos pa'i) bsláb-bya (bslab bya) useful doctrines Glr.; dgós-pa (dgos pa) yin (yin) or yod (yod) B. and C., *gó-śes (*go shes) yod* (yod*) W. it is requisite; dgas(-pa) (dgas(-pa)) med (med) B., *gó-gyu (*go gyu) meṇ* (men*) C., *gó-śe (*go she) man* (man*) or *med* (*med*) W., it is unnecessary, unfit, not wanted; mi-dgós-pa (mi dgos pa) useless, noxious, mi-dgós-pai (mi dgos pa'i) ₒpra-mén ('phra men) pernicious witchcraft Pth.; dgos-byéd (dgos byed) useful, don (don) dgos-byéd (dgos byed) ci (ci) ₒdug ('dug) what there is in it of useful contents Mil.; dgos-ₒdód (dgos 'dod) wishes and wants, dgos-ₒdód (dgos 'dod) byuṅ-bai (byung ba'i) dpal (dpal) a treasure out of which all wishes and wants come, i. e. are satisfied Glr.; dgos-ₒdód (dgos 'dod) nags-tsál (nags tshal) a forest for wishes, i.e. a forest which grants every wish; dgos-ₒdód (dgos 'dod) necessary expenses Cs.
to bend, to be curving or crooked; dbyibs (dbyibs) dgyé-ba (dgye ba) stooping, cringing, ducking S.g.
glu (glu) dgyér-ba (dgyer ba) for glu (glu) lén-pa (len pa) to sing, chant, expression of the Bonpas; the word is also pronounced *ghyér-wa* (*ghyer wa*).

resp. for dgá-ba (dga' ba), to rejoice, to be glad; often with ṭugs (thugs): rgyál-poi (rgyal po'i) (or -pọ (-po)) ṭugs (thugs) dgyes (dgyes) the king rejoiced; with la (la) (to rejoice) at or in, (to be glad) of; to please, to be pleased, to choose, ió-bo (jo bo) ₒbyón-pa-la ('byon pa la) ṭugs-dgyés-par (thugs dgyes par) ₒdug ('dug) it seems the lord is pleased to walk Glr.; mi (mi) dgyés-te (dgyes te) sorrowful, sad, discouraged, dejected; angry, indignant; cf. dgá-ba (dga' ba).
to bend, to double down Sch., v. dgyé-ba (dgye ba).

also dgrá-bo (dgra bo), Ssk. शत्रु 1. enemy, foe. sdáṅ-bai (sdang ba'i) dgra (dgra) the hating enemy, (opp. to byáms-pai (byams pa'i) ynyén (gnyen)), frq. used of imaginary hostile powers, that are to be attacked and withstood only by witchcraft; dgra (dgra) ynyen (gnyen) med (med) there is no difference between friend and enemy = no such thing exists (viz in the golden age); dgrar (dgrar) ₒgyúr-ba ('gyur ba) to become an enemy (to one) Tar.; dgra (dgra) byéd-pa (byed pa), dgrá-ru (dgra ru) ldáṅ-ba (ldang ba), láṅ-ba (lang ba) to act in a hostile manner, la (la), against; dgra (dgra) slóṅ-ba (slong ba), causative form, to make a person one's enemy S.g.; dgrar (dgrar) sém-pa (sem pa), ₒdzín-pa ('dzin pa) to look upon one as an enemy, to take him for an enemy; dgrar (dgrar) śés-pa (shes pa) id.; dgrá-bcòm-pa (dgra bcom pa) Arhant, Arhat, the most perfect Buddhist saint (Ssk. अर्हत् venerable; the Buddhists, however, explain it as a compound of ari enemy and han to extirpate, he who has extirpated the enemies i.e. the passions Burn. I. 295, II. 287. Köpp. I. 400). Also dgra (dgra) bgegs (bgegs) ₒdúl-ba ('dul ba) Glr. is interpreted as referring to the subduing of spiritual enemies. — sṅá-dgrá (snga dgra) a former foe, dá-dgra (da dgra) a present foe, pẏí-dgra (phyi dgra) a future foe Cs.; pẏi-dgra (phyi dgra) prob. also a foreign enemy. — ₒcí-dgra ('chi dgra) a mortal, deadly enemy Cs. — dgrá-ca (dgra cha) weapon, arms Wdn., dgrá-sta (dgra sta) battle-axe; dgrá-lha (dgra lha) v. lha (lha). — 2. In W. also punishment, *kó-la (kho la) ḍa (da) ṗog (phog) soṅ* (song*) he was punished; also for any self-incurred misfortune: *kyód-la (khyod la) ḍa (da) ṗog (phog) yin* (yin*) you will draw upon yourself trouble, fatal consequences.
Cs. another form for gégs-pa (gegs pa).
to laugh Dzl., cf. dgód-pa (dgod pa).
